channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Dolton, Illinois, mayor Tiffany Henyard projected to lose primary in landside
item.description: Dolton, Illinois, mayor Tiffany Henyard, a Democrat, is projected to lose in a landslide in the village's mayoral primary held on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man who was mad about Chinese spy balloon faces sentencing for threatening ex-Speaker McCarthy
item.description: A Montana man faces years in federal prison at his sentencing on Wednesday for threatening to assault former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge OKs prison abuse settlement, rejecting Trump administration's push to rewrite protections
item.description: A judge has given final approval to a legal settlement that subjects the federal Bureau of Prisons to independent monitoring and increased transparency in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse at a now-shuttered federal women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to release billions of dollars in blocked foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court order to ease its funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration creates registry for immigrants who are in the US illegally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is creating a registry for all people who are in the United States illegally, and those who don't self-report could face fines or prosecution, immigration officials announced Tuesday. Everyone who is in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans pass budget resolution, clearing a key early test for Trump agenda
item.description: The GOP framework that passed Tuesday allows the party to move ahead on a sweeping multitrillion plan to address defense, energy, immigration and tax policy.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: Digital license plates allowed under proposal in the Nevada Legislature
item.description: A new bill at the Nevada Legislature would allow the DMV to accept digital license plates purchased from an approved vendor.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cicero Primary Election: Incumbent Dominick defeats Rodriguez, will serve 5th straight term
item.description: Incumbent Cicero Town President Larry Dominick will serve a fifth straight four-year term, defeating Esteban Rodriguez in the primary election Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rebik, WGN 9

item.title: Candidates for NYC mayor work to get on the ballot as Cuomo looms large
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Tuesday was the first day when New York City mayoral candidates could collect signatures to gain access to the primary ballot in June. Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo continues to be favored in the race for mayor, despite not officially declaring.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he will offer 'gold cards' for $5 million path to citizenship, replacing investor visas
item.description: President Donald Trump says he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: “We're going to be selling a gold card. You have a green card. This is a gold card. We're going to be putting a price on that card of about $5 million and that's going to give you green card privileges,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi warns states to comply with Trump order on transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi warned officials in Maine, California and Minnesota on Tuesday to comply with President Trump’s executive orders to bar transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports, promising swift legal action.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Upheaval and firings at CDC raise fears about disease outbreak response
item.description: Staff and observers worry that the agency may not be prepared for emerging threats including bird flu and insect-borne diseases.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Canadians panic over 'American oppression' after discovering longstanding Google Maps parks designation
item.description: Google is working to update its map system to reflect Canada's "provincial park" designations, removing "state park" designations for the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court throws out Oklahoma inmate Richard Glossip's murder conviction and death sentence
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday threw out the murder conviction and death penalty for Richard Glossip, an Oklahoma man who was found guilty in the killing of a motel owner but has steadfastly maintained his innocence and averted multiple attempts by the state to execute him.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jon Stewart cuts hand during fiery monologue on DOGE, prescription drugs
item.description: Jon Stewart is railing against sweeping cuts by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) — and giving his hand one of its own in the process — during an impassioned monologue on "The Daily Show.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Canada and Mexico tariffs are 'going forward' with more import taxes to come
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that his tariffs on Canada and Mexico are starting next month, ending a monthlong suspension on the planned import taxes that could potentially hurt economic growth and worsen inflation.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FABIOLA SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt change as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans have muscled their budget blueprint to passage, with a push from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Kevin Freking And Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Court ruling lets White House ban on the Associated Press continue, for now
item.description: The Trump administration may continue — for now — to keep the AP from covering key events. A federal judge declined to issue a temporary restraining order on Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s Cabinet members have already backtracked on some promises made before being confirmed
item.description: As they mustered support for their confirmations by the U.S. Senate, some of President Donald Trump’s appointees made statements from which they’ve already distanced themselves upon taking office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: St. Pete lawmaker proposes bill to dive into wastewater, sewage spill concerns
item.description: The devastating impacts of storm surge and flood waters are a harsh reality many Floridians know all too well.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Clint Hill, Secret Service agent who leaped onto JFK’s car after the president was shot, dies at 93
item.description: Hill was born in 1932 and grew up in Washburn, N.D. He attended Concordia College in Moorhead, Minn., served in the Army and worked as a railroad agent before joining the Secret Service in 1958.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As time change nears, Nevada considers opting out of daylight saving
item.description: Don't have a heart attack, but daylight saving time is less than two weeks away. If a Nevada lawmaker has her way, it will be the last time the state "springs ahead.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 years on, here’s what to keep an eye on in the Ukraine war
item.description: Thousands of civilians have been killed since Russia launched its full-scale invasion on Feb. 24, 2022. Amid a stark shift in U.S. policy, Ukrainians want the war to end — but not on Russia’s terms.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes, Joanna Kakissis, and Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Roosevelt Hotel to shut down as migrant arrival center: NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – The migrant arrival center at the Roosevelt Hotel in Manhattan is set to shut down, New York City Mayor Eric Adams will announce on Monday. More than 173,000 migrants have gone through the site since it opened in May 2023, according to city data.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers sue Elon Musk over email ultimatum
item.description: The lawsuit seeks to block mass layoffs, including any connected to an email distributed by the Office of Personnel Management on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk blurs the line between his government and business roles
item.description: The tech titan and President Trump say they will avoid any conflicts of interest, but it’s difficult for the public to verify that.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: PIX Panel discusses Hochul’s safeguard plan for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Thursday announced proposed safeguards to ensure that New York City is operating in the best interest of its residents.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Dexter's Law: Pushing for tougher sentencing for animal cruelty
item.description: One Tampa Bay area lawmaker is pushing to toughen sentences for animal cruelty, specifically in cases like the shocking discovery of Dexter, a decapitated dog found at Fort DeSoto Beach.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
